chapter_context.summary: Rebukes the faithless shepherds, announces the Righteous Branch (The LORD Our Righteousness), and fiercely denounces the lies of false prophets.

outline.text: Rebuking Shepherds; Promise of Righteous Branch
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rebuking Shepherds; Promise of Righteous Branch (23:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Deep Grief over Profane Priests and Lies of Prophets
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Deep Grief over Profane Priests and Lies of Prophets (23:9-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: God's Word as Fire and Hammer; Confronting Dream-tellers
outline.explanation: This section focuses on God's Word as Fire and Hammer; Confronting Dream-tellers (23:23-32), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Burden of the Lord Mocked and Severely Rebuked
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Burden of the Lord Mocked and Severely Rebuked (23:33-40), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Warning
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of warning, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Weeping Prophet
key_themes.description: Jeremiah's deep emotional connection to the message and the people, illustrating the heart of God breaking for His children's rebellion.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and historical accounts of Chapter 23, in what ways does Jeremiah's call to faithfulness encourage you in your own walk today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose covenant obedience and trust in God's promises of restoration over temporary security?

cultural_historical.title: The Fall of Jerusalem and the Exile
cultural_historical.text: The historical backdrop of Jeremiah's ministry, witnessing the gradual collapse of Judah under the Babylonian Empire and the destruction of the Temple.
